compiled_state.title: Help claims story/epic close 'requires --verdict-hash' but the gate is opt-in (default off)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Help text asserts story/epic close "requires --verdict-hash (or --force-close)" but the verdict gate is opt-in and OFF by default — agents are misled into supplying ceremony that is not required (or worse, assuming closes are gated when they are not).

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. S=$(rebar create story "a story" | tail -1); rebar claim $S
2. rebar transition $S in_progress closed -> exit 0, no --verdict-hash, no --force-close.
3. rebar help transition -> "story/epic close requires --verdict-hash (or --force-close=<reason>)".

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: help states the gate fires only when verify.require_verdict_for_close=true in .rebar/config.conf.
- Actual: unconditional "requires" in two places.

## Source
- Gate: src/rebar/_engine/ticket_txn.py:102-114 (require_verdict = False default; opt-in per commit abb6f23).
- Misleading text: src/rebar/_engine/rebar:94 and src/rebar/_engine/ticket-transition.sh:38.

## Fix direction
Reword both strings to "required when verify.require_verdict_for_close is enabled (.rebar/config.conf)". Doc-only change.

## Suggested RED test
String assertion on rebar help transition output naming the config key (RED while the old wording stands).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both help strings name the config key and the opt-in default (RED string test first)
- [ ] README/CLAUDE.md verdict-gate mentions audited for the same overstatement

verify_commands.command: bash -c 'rebar help transition | grep -q require_verdict_for_close && echo PASS'
verify_commands.dd_text: help names the config key, not an unconditional requirement
